rulings.Case Title: SAM GLEASON et al VS. SEEMA HOSSAIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From Defts
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 1, 2010, line 2, PLAINTIFFS SAM GLEASON, DEBORAH NELSON'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From Defendants. GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. REQUESTS 6 AND 9 ARE DENIED AS OVERBROAD AND IMPLICATING PRIVILEGED DOCUMENTS. ALSO, RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS HAVE BEEN PRODUCED AND PLAINTIFFS HAVE ACCESS TO THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD. REQUESTS 31 AND 32 ARE GRANTED IF ANY ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS EXIST. REDACTED STATEMENTS HAVE BEEN PRODUCED. REQUESTS 40 AND 41 ARE GRANTED. REQUESTS 42 AND 43 ARE DENIED AS OVERBROAD AND IMPLICATING ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE. REQUEST 45 DENIED, NO DOCUMENTS EXIST. REQUEST 46 DENIED AS OVERBROAD. REQUEST 47 DENIED AS OVERBROAD AND NO DOCUMENTS EXIST. REQUESTS 52 AND 53 DENIED AS OVERBROAD AND ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE, AND ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RIKKI B GATES VS. INNER CIRCLE ENTERTAINMENT IV A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions; Request For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 1, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT TAZ HAYNES Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions. DENIED. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO PAY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $4,312.50 (11.5 HOURS AT $375/HOUR.) MOTION BROUGHT WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION AND DEFENSE COUNSEL FAILED TO CONFER AS REQUIRED. (CCP SECTION 2023.020) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. VS. PINN BROTHERS CONSTRUCTION, INC., et al*****TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CLARA*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 1, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT PINN BROTHERS CONSTRUCTION, INC., ALAN PINN Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment. GRANT. DEFENDANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS VOID. COURT CONSTRUES THE MOTION AS AN ATTORNEY AFFIDAVIT OF FAULT MOTION. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL TO PAY $1,000 TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AS REASONABLE FEES/SANCTIONS. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 1, 2010, line 6, RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Re Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs' CEQA Cause Of Action Pursuant To California Public Resources Code Section 21167.4 - DUPLICATE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WENDY ROBINSON et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al (CEQA Case)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs' Ceqa Cause Of Action Pursuant To California Public Resources Code Section 211677.4
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 1, 2010, line 6, RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Re Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs' CEQA Cause Of Action Pursuant To California Public Resources Code Section 21167.4. DENIED. PETITIONERS PRESENTED A REQUEST FOR A HEARING TO THE COURT CLERK ON 2/5/10, THE SAME DAY THEY FILED THEIR CEQA PETITION. THE REQUEST FULLY COMPLIED WITH PUBLIC RESOURCES CODE SECTION 21167.4 AND THE CLERK ERRED IN REFUSING TO ACCEPT THE PETITION AND FILE IT. PETITIONERS REQUEST FOR A HEARING IS DEEMED FILED ON 2/5/10 IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE CODE. IT IS THE LEGISLATIVE AND JUDICIAL POLICY OF THIS STATE TO PREFER DISPOSITION OF LITIGATION ON THE MERITS RATHER THAN ON PROCEDURAL GROUNDS. (SEE CARLSON V. DEPT. OF FISH & GAME (1998) 68 CAL.APP. 4TH 1268.) =(302/CWW)
